This run was an initial draft of the pipeline adaption.
The pipeline was updated to include blocking and matching configurations for three dataset pairs: kaggle_small with uber_eats_small, kaggle_small with yelp_small, and uber_eats_small with yelp_small.

Blocking strategies were set as semantic similarity using EmbeddingBlocker for kaggle_small-uber_eats_small and uber_eats_small-yelp_small pairs, and exact match multi-column blocking using StandardBlocker for kaggle_small-yelp_small.
Matching comparators were defined for each dataset pair, including string comparators with various similarity functions and preprocessing, and numeric comparators with specified max differences.
Feature extractors were created for each dataset pair using the defined comparators.
Ground truth correspondences were loaded for ML training for each dataset pair.
Features were extracted for training using the feature extractors and ground truth data.
Training datasets were prepared by selecting feature columns and labels.
GridSearchCV was set up with multiple classifiers and parameter grids to select the best model for each dataset pair.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a column-specific evaluation function registration within a DataFusionStrategy object named 'restaurant_fusion_eval_strategy'.

Evaluation functions were grouped by field families such as tokenized_match for textual fields (e.g., name, city, state), set_equality_match for set-like fields (e.g., categories and other list-like columns), and numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields (e.g., rating, latitude, longitude).
Helper functions were added for parsing source IDs and detecting gold ID prefixes to build an evaluation view aligning fused output with gold standard data.
List-like columns were detected and normalized before evaluation to ensure consistent comparison.
ID alignment diagnostics were added to measure coverage between fused IDs and gold standard IDs.
Evaluation results were outputted with detailed per-attribute metrics and overall precision, recall, and F1 scores.
Debugging support was enabled with output to a JSONL debug file.
Support for loading XML and CSV data formats was included.

The dominant evaluation strategy used was a column-specific evaluation function registration using tokenized_match, set_equality_match, and numeric_tolerance_match functions applied to relevant columns.
Fields related to names and addresses were evaluated using tokenized_match.
Fields representing categories and other list-like columns were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Numeric fields such as rating, rating_count, latitude, and longitude were evaluated using numeric_tolerance_match.
The gold standard fusion test set loading method was changed from load_xml to load_csv to correctly handle the CSV input file format.
The evaluation print formatting was adjusted to safely handle None values for precision, recall, and F1 scores by printing 'nan' instead of failing.
The import statement was updated to remove load_xml since it was no longer used.
The evaluation script included list-like column detection and normalization to improve evaluation consistency.
The prior run failed due to attempting to load a CSV file with load_xml, causing an error.
Switching to load_csv fixed the input data loading issue, enabling the evaluation to proceed.

overall accuracy is 75.92%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
Phone number fields (phone_e164 and phone_raw) are completely broken in evaluation, showing 0.0 accuracy despite fused values being numerically identical to expected values.

There are secondary issues with cluster ID (_id) and source attribution accuracy being moderate to low.

This run is a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation feedback.
The fusion strategy was changed to use a custom phone fuser for the phone_raw and phone_e164 attributes instead of the previous longest_string fuser.
A new kaggle_id_fuser was introduced for the kaggle380k_id attribute to better handle ID fusion semantics.
ID columns were separated by source to avoid overloading kaggle380k_id across datasets, introducing source-specific ID columns and a unified technical _source_id column for blocking and matching.
Singleton retention was disabled and lineage fields were set to include source_id.
The fusion behavior shifted from using longest_string for phone fields to a specialized phone_fuser that normalizes phone numbers to consistent string formats before fusion.
The kaggle380k_id attribute fusion shifted from longest_string to a dedicated kaggle_id_fuser to correctly handle canonical cluster IDs and avoid mixing source IDs.
The pipeline now uses a technical unified ID column (_source_id) for blocking and matching instead of the overloaded kaggle380k_id.
Phone fields are normalized by stripping trailing '.0' and non-digit characters before fusion and CSV output.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was to improve evaluation accuracy by fixing schema and type mismatches, especially for phone number fields and ID alignment.
Phone number fields ('phone_e164' and 'phone_raw') were identified as having systematic evaluation mismatches due to numeric vs string type differences and formatting issues.
ID fields, especially 'kaggle380k_id', suffered from misfusion and misalignment because the field was used as a universal ID across datasets, causing evaluation mismatches.
Most other attributes such as 'name', 'city', 'state', 'postal_code', 'latitude', and 'longitude' showed strong accuracy (0.95-0.98).
Secondary issues were noted in cluster ID ('_id') and source attribution fields.
Added custom phone number normalization and matching evaluation functions to handle numeric-to-string conversion issues and formatting differences.
Modified the evaluation view building logic to prefer canonical Kaggle IDs for alignment, falling back to source IDs or cluster IDs as needed.
Enabled XML loading support for the gold standard dataset instead of CSV loading.
Added list-like column detection and normalization to improve evaluation consistency.
